message:
feat(core): abstract IPC and storage layers over runtime_compat for asupersync portability

Replaces all direct tokio-specific async primitives in the IPC server/client
and storage modules with crate::runtime_compat abstractions, enabling these
subsystems to run on both the tokio and asupersync cooperative runtimes.

ipc.rs (881 lines changed):
- Replaced tokio::task::JoinSet with runtime_compat::task::JoinSet for
  tracking active IPC connection tasks in the server accept loop.
- Replaced tokio::spawn with runtime_compat::task::spawn for spawning
  per-connection handler tasks.
- Replaced tokio::task::JoinHandle return types with runtime_compat
  equivalents in start_auth_server() and other helpers.
- Rewrote all async IPC tests from #[tokio::test] to a new
  run_async_test() helper that constructs a RuntimeBuilder::current_thread()
  runtime, allowing tests to run under either runtime backend.
- Imported CompatRuntime and RuntimeBuilder from runtime_compat for the
  test harness.

storage.rs (70 lines changed):
- Added spawn_blocking_storage<T, F>() private async helper method that
  dispatches to asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking or
  tokio::task::spawn_blocking based on the active feature flag, with
  appropriate error mapping for each backend.
- Replaced 3+ direct tokio::task::spawn_blocking call sites
  (with_config initialization, get_event_annotations, and others) with
  Self::spawn_blocking_storage(), eliminating redundant cfg blocks and
  error-mapping boilerplate at each call site.

snapshot_engine.rs (219 lines changed):
- Refactored the nested block-with-cfg patterns introduced in the
  previous asupersync commit (ec52fb31) to a cleaner cfg-gated let-binding
  style. Instead of wrapping each spawn_blocking call in a { #[cfg(..)]
  { ... } #[cfg(not(..))] { ... } } block, each call site now uses
  two adjacent #[cfg(..)] let statements, reducing indentation depth
  and improving readability across all 5 spawn_blocking call sites.

runtime_compat.rs (16 new lines):
- Added mpsc_send() and mpsc_recv_option() convenience helper tests
  that verify the send/recv wrappers round-trip correctly and return
  None when the sender is dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
